name: Victor Feldman
description: English recording artist; jazz musician, vibraphonist (1934–1987)
abstract: Victor Stanley Feldman (7 April 1934 – 12 May 1987) was an English jazz musician who played mainly piano, vibraphone, and percussion. He began performing professionally during childhood, eventually earning acclaim in the UK jazz scene as an adult.

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Hardaway
about_quotes.evidence: And also on that band was another famous guy, Victor Feldman had just come over... So that made the band sort of a little special. And then in the little band Victor played drums.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Hardaway interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 14, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Ron Carter
about_quotes.evidence: Cannonball had the band of him, Nat, Louis Hayes and I think Victor Feldman. ... I was the bass player for this one tune a night. We had a 2 ½ week tour of Europe.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ron Carter — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Magnusson
about_quotes.evidence: And it was Victor Feldman and John Guerin did it also, on drums, we played a quartet.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Magnusson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Roger Kellaway
about_quotes.evidence: I became part of the L.A. Express... Victor Feldman took my place
about_quotes.doc_title: Roger Kellaway interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 14, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Harold Mabern
about_quotes.evidence: So as it turns out Victor Feldman did the opening night. ... I was prepared to get to the gig and for him to say that he may have changed his mind ... during that time I was talking to Mulgrew ... that was from April 1 until about April 24, man.
about_quotes.doc_title: Harold Mabern interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 31, 2010

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: I thought Victor Feldman was fantastic. I’m glad you’re mentioning these people, because he went and died when he shouldn’t have died.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
